mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound sustains low discount rates on equity cash flows, mechanically supporting valuations and producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following quarter regardless of short-term news flow.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate declines by more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window from an elevated post-shock level, it signals accelerating labor market repair, which raises consumption expectations and drives S&P 500 gains over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls > 0.3pp over trailing 3 months while UNRATE level > 5%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=6.7 elevated relative to pre-shock baseline
failure_condition: In periods meeting the trigger, forward 20-day SPX returns are negative or indistinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.05) across at least two independent historical episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates broad-based consumer and investment demand recovery that is not captured by either series alone; simultaneous positive momentum in both predicts above-trend S&P 500 returns over the next month because it signals durable-goods and services demand reinforcing each other.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change > 0 AND HOUST 3-month change > 0 simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=427186 and HOUST=880.0 both consistent with recovery phase
failure_condition: Joint-positive RSAFS/HOUST momentum episodes produce forward 20-day SPX returns statistically indistinguishable from single-series baselines, indicating no true interaction effect (interaction coefficient not significant at p<0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery regime, month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects reactivation of supply chains and manufacturing capacity, which precedes broader earnings growth and drives S&P 500 returns higher over the subsequent quarter; this effect is specific to recovery phases and reverses in late-cycle overheating.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=101.0251 consistent with early-stage rebound off a supply shock trough
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum fails to correlate with positive 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03) once the recovery regime is confirmed via GDPC1 turning positive for two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) is a always-on leading indicator of near-term consumer income and spending capacity, so any positive payroll print predicts modestly positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week via improved short-term earnings sentiment.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=137699 thousand persons, monthly series with high revision risk
failure_condition: Sign of monthly PAYEMS change shows no significant association with sign of forward 5-day SPX return (directional hit rate not different from 50% at p>0.10) over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI (CPIAUCSL) accelerates such that its year-over-year rate crosses above a threshold implying imminent policy tightening, markets price in future rate hikes ahead of any FEDFUNDS move, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY change accelerates by > 0.5pp over 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL=234.933 as latest reading, low revision risk series
failure_condition: Triggered CPI-acceleration episodes show positive or flat forward 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances across the historical sample.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between an ultra-low federal funds rate and a still-elevated unemployment rate identifies a slack-driven policy-accommodation regime in which markets anticipate prolonged stimulus; the combination of low FEDFUNDS and high UNRATE jointly predicts stronger S&P 500 gains over the following quarter than either variable predicts alone, because it signals both cheap capital and headroom for non-inflationary growth.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.07 and UNRATE=6.7 jointly present in snapshot
regime_hint: accommodative policy with labor market slack
failure_condition: The joint FEDFUNDS-low/UNRATE-high condition produces 60-day forward returns no better than the unconditional historical average, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p>0.10) when regressed against forward returns controlling for each series individually.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime defined by a rising federal funds rate, a month-over-month contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) signals that higher financing costs are already curbing real output, foreshadowing earnings downgrades and driving S&P 500 returns down over the next trading week; this mechanism is inactive when FEDFUNDS is near zero.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < 0 while FEDFUNDS is rising for 2+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=101.0251 and FEDFUNDS=0.07 currently near zero, mechanism dormant in current snapshot
failure_condition: During confirmed tightening cycles, INDPRO contractions are not followed by negative forward 5-day SPX returns more often than the unconditional base rate (binomial test p>0.10).
